Job description
Full Time / 37 Hours Per Week / Permanent
Full Time / 37 Hours Per Week / Fixed Term for 18 months
Are you inquisitive, analytical, and creative, with strong problem solving skills? Then you may be interested in joining the Social Care and Education Project Management Office as a Business Analyst.
You’ll work with Business Change Commissioning Managers, Business Analysts and supporting staff in a dynamic, well-established team; playing a key role in delivering improvements to existing systems and services within Social Care and Education across Leicester.
The role is hybrid, meaning you can work from home and/or in a council office, with the expectation that you work from a city council office at least twice a week. When working in the office, you’ll be based at Halford House, but may be required to travel across the city to undertake visits and attend meetings.
We have full time positions working 37 hours a week, Monday to Friday. One on a permanent basis and one on a fixed term contract for 18 months. We offer flexible and agile working to promote a healthy work-life balance.
For Leicester City Council employees, we will consider the fixed term vacancy on a secondment basis. If you wish to apply for this post on a secondment basis then please make your line manager aware before applying.
What you’ll be doing
Assisting in the development of business cases and implementation plans, you’ll identify and make recommendations about risks, issues, benefits, and requirements. Working with a range of internal stakeholders and external partners, you’ll investigate business functions, processes, information flows and data structures. Using business improvement tools, you’ll apply methodical and consistent techniques, to undertake thorough analysis. You’ll attend and often chair meetings to present issues and solutions. You’ll specify and document business requirements ensuring traceability back to source, and challenge positively when required.
Like Patrick, a Business Analyst in the team you can progress within this role. See below what he says about the role:
"As a Business Analyst within this team, you will be working in a dynamic and complex environment. This will involve working with a mixture of qualitative and quantitative data sets.
This role will test your ability to not only analyse data but to analyse the business itself. As part of working with this team I have developed significantly in stakeholder management.
Within the role I have had to handle varying projects in order to understand the root causes of issues that have been identified across Social Care & Educa
